query: What is the specified concrete strength?
answer:
The specified concrete strength varies by structural element as outlined in the structural specifications. For footings, the minimum strength is 3,000 psi at 28 days; for columns, it is 5,000 psi at 28 days; for floor slabs, it is 4,000 psi at 28 days; and for post-tensioned slabs, it is 5,000 psi at 28 days with high early strength. This information is sourced from STRUCT_SPEC.txt, Section 03 30 00 - Cast-in-Place Concrete.

**DISCLAIMER:** AI-generated. Review by qualified engineer required.
**Sources:** ARCH_DRAWING_NOTES.txt, STRUCT_SPEC.txt

sources.text:
PROJECT: DOWNTOWN OFFICE TOWER - STRUCTURAL SPECIFICATIONS
SECTION 03 30 00 - CAST-IN-PLACE CONCRETE

1.1 GENERAL
All concrete work shall comply with ACI 318-19 and local building codes. 1.2 CONCRETE MIX DESIGNS
- Footings: 3,000 psi minimum at 28 days
- Columns: 5,000 psi minimum at 28 days  
- Floor slabs: 4,000 psi minimum at 28 days
- Post-tensioned slabs: 5,000 psi minimum at 28 days, high early strength

1.3 REINFORCING STEEL
All reinforcing shall be ASTM A615 Grade 60.

PROJECT: DOWNTOWN OFFICE TOWER - STRUCTURAL SPECIFICATIONS
SECTION 03 30 00 - CAST-IN-PLACE CONCRETE

1.1 GENERAL
All concrete work shall comply with ACI 318-19 and local building codes. 1.2 CONCRETE MIX DESIGNS
- Footings: 3,000 psi minimum at 28 days
- Columns: 5,000 psi minimum at 28 days  
- Floor slabs: 4,000 psi minimum at 28 days
- Post-tensioned slabs: 5,000 psi minimum at 28 days, high early strength

1.3 REINFORCING STEEL
All reinforcing shall be ASTM A615 Grade 60.

1.4 FIREPROOFING
Structural steel columns and beams shall receive 2-hour fireproofing per IBC Table 601. Fireproofing material: Spray-applied cementitious, minimum 1.5 inches thick

1.5 STRUCTURAL STEEL
Wide flange beams: ASTM A992, Grade 50
Connection design per AISC 360-16

1.6 DEAD LOADS
Floor finish: 2 psf
Partitions: 15 psf (movable)
Mechanical/electrical: 10 psf
Ceiling: 3 psf

1.7 LIVE LOADS
Office areas: 50 psf
Corridors: 100 psf
Lobby/Atrium: 100 psf
Mechanical rooms: 150 psf

1.4 FIREPROOFING
Structural steel columns and beams shall receive 2-hour fireproofing per IBC Table 601. Fireproofing material: Spray-applied cementitious, minimum 1.5 inches thick

1.5 STRUCTURAL STEEL
Wide flange beams: ASTM A992, Grade 50
Connection design per AISC 360-16

1.6 DEAD LOADS
Floor finish: 2 psf
Partitions: 15 psf (movable)
Mechanical/electrical: 10 psf
Ceiling: 3 psf

1.7 LIVE LOADS
Office areas: 50 psf
Corridors: 100 psf
Lobby/Atrium: 100 psf
Mechanical rooms: 150 psf

ACCESSIBILITY
All design shall comply with ADA 2010 Standards and local accessibility codes. Minimum clear door width: 32 inches
Maximum ramp slope: 1:12

query: What is the required fire protection rating?
answer:
The required fire protection rating for structural steel columns and beams is a 2-hour fireproofing per IBC Table 601, as specified in STRUCT_SPEC.txt under section 1.4 FIREPROOFING. This is achieved using spray-applied cementitious material with a minimum thickness of 1.5 inches. No conflicting information was found in the provided documents. (Source: STRUCT_SPEC.txt)

**DISCLAIMER:** AI-generated. Review by qualified engineer required.
**Sources:** FIRE_PROTECTION_SPEC.txt, STRUCT_SPEC.txt, MECH_SPEC_HVAC.txt

PROJECT: DOWNTOWN OFFICE TOWER - FIRE PROTECTION SPECIFICATIONS
SECTION 21 13 13 - WET PIPE SPRINKLER SYSTEMS

1.1 GENERAL
All fire protection systems shall comply with NFPA 13 (2022 Edition) and local AHJ requirements.

PROJECT: DOWNTOWN OFFICE TOWER - FIRE PROTECTION SPECIFICATIONS
SECTION 21 13 13 - WET PIPE SPRINKLER SYSTEMS

1.1 GENERAL
All fire protection systems shall comply with NFPA 13 (2022 Edition) and local AHJ requirements.

Main trunk velocity: 1,800 FPM maximum
Branch velocity: 1,200 FPM maximum
Minimum duct size: 6 inches diameter

1.5 FIRE PROTECTION COORDINATION
HVAC ducts penetrating fire-rated assemblies shall have fire dampers per NFPA 90A. Smoke dampers required at all smoke barrier penetrations.

PROJECT: DOWNTOWN OFFICE TOWER - FIRE PROTECTION SPECIFICATIONS
SECTION 21 13 13 - WET PIPE SPRINKLER SYSTEMS

query: What is the specified concrete strength?
answer:
The specified concrete strength varies by structural element as outlined in the structural specifications. For footings, the minimum strength is 3,000 psi at 28 days; for columns, it is 5,000 psi at 28 days; for floor slabs, it is 4,000 psi at 28 days; and for post-tensioned slabs, it is 5,000 psi at 28 days with high early strength. This information is sourced from STRUCT_SPEC.txt, Section 03 30 00 - Cast-in-Place Concrete.

**DISCLAIMER:** AI-generated. Review by qualified engineer required.

PROJECT: DOWNTOWN OFFICE TOWER - STRUCTURAL SPECIFICATIONS
SECTION 03 30 00 - CAST-IN-PLACE CONCRETE

1.1 GENERAL
All concrete work shall comply with ACI 318-19 and local building codes. 1.2 CONCRETE MIX DESIGNS
- Footings: 3,000 psi minimum at 28 days
- Columns: 5,000 psi minimum at 28 days  
- Floor slabs: 4,000 psi minimum at 28 days
- Post-tensioned slabs: 5,000 psi minimum at 28 days, high early strength

1.3 REINFORCING STEEL
All reinforcing shall be ASTM A615 Grade 60.

1.1 GENERAL
